claims_verified.claim: All in-tree callers of make_runner use the new (config, role) signature; no stale 1-arg caller left
claims_verified.evidence: grep 'make_runner' across orchestrator/*.py and tests/*.py — every call site is deps.make_runner(config, "<role>") or lambda c, role: ...; none use the old 1-arg shape

claims_verified.claim: Suite is green independently re-run (not trusting rw1's numbers)
claims_verified.evidence: ruff check . -> 'All checks passed!'; basedpyright -> '0 errors, 1548 warnings, 0 notes'; pytest -q -> '686 passed in 119.71s'

claims_verified.claim: No .laddy/specs/*.md changes on this branch (divergence artifact, not scope creep, per rw1)
claims_verified.evidence: git log origin/main..HEAD -- '.laddy/specs/*.md' is empty; merge-base c8893d7 predates origin/main HEAD 0b7f1bd

claims_verified.claim: set_model_flag handles a trailing bare '--model' (no value) without crashing or corrupting the command
claims_verified.evidence: agents.py:148-161 loop condition 'i + 1 < len(parts)' skips a valueless trailing flag and falls through to append; tests/test_agents.py:139-143 test_set_model_flag_trailing_model_without_value_appends covers it

claims_verified.claim: Invalid ROLE_*_VENDOR is fail-closed (ConfigError) before any OrchestratorConfig is constructed, no partial state
claims_verified.evidence: config.py:100-105 raises inside _parse_role_bindings, which from_env calls before returning; test_role_binding_invalid_vendor_raises passes

claims_verified.claim: clarify role's runner resolves to byte-for-byte the same command as before this change (AC1)
claims_verified.evidence: run.py:231 now calls deps.make_runner(config, "clarify"); _legacy_role_cmd's else-branch returns config.claude_cmd or DEFAULT_CLAUDE_CMD, identical to the old clarify-shared lambda

claims_verified.claim: A role-name typo in ROLE_<NAME>_* env (e.g. ROLE_DEV_VENDOR instead of ROLE_DEVELOPER_VENDOR) parses into an unused binding under the wrong role key and is silently never applied
claims_verified.evidence: config.py's _ROLE_ENV_RE matches any [A-Za-z0-9]+ role segment by design (spec requires future roles need no code change); run.py only queries config.role_bindings.get(role) for the five known role strings, so a mis-spelled role key sits inert

findings.summary: Generic ROLE_<NAME>_* parsing accepts any role spelling, so an operator typo (ROLE_DEV_VENDOR vs ROLE_DEVELOPER_VENDOR) silently produces an inert binding instead of an error or warning.

test_assessment: AC1-AC5 are each covered by a dedicated, appropriately named test in tests/test_role_runner.py, and I confirmed the assertions actually exercise the claimed code paths (not just call it) by reading both the test and the implementation side by side. Failure-mode coverage is present for the new pure functions: set_model_flag (empty cmd, trailing bare --model, replace vs append) and _parse_role_bindings (blank value ignored, unknown knob, non-ROLE-prefixed near miss, invalid vendor, partial fields). I independently re-ran ruff, basedpyright, and pytest rather than trusting the reported numbers, and got the same results (686 passed, 0 type errors).
residual_risks: codex '-c model_reasoning_effort=<v>' flag correctness (accepted by 'codex exec --full-auto -c ...') is not exercised against a live codex binary in any test — rests on documented CLI convention, same residual risk rw1 already flagged.

Role-name typos in ROLE_*_VENDOR/MODEL/THINKING are silently inert rather than erroring, by design (spec wants unknown future roles to parse with no code change) — an operator misconfiguration risk, not a code defect.
